name: El Lucero – circular from Puerto Blanquillo - Parque Natural Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
name_original: El Lucero – circular desde Puerto Blanquillo - Parque Natural Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
region_intro: Hiking around Canillas De Albaida offers access to diverse mountain landscapes within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. The region features pine forests, canyons, and peaks, with many routes providing views of the surrounding terrain. Several rivers and streams, including the Rio Turvilla, Rio Cajula, and Arroyo de la Cueva, are integral to the area's natural environment. The terrain includes various hills and elevated points, providing varied hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Canillas De Albaida The most popular hiking route is El Lucero – circular from Puerto Blanquillo - Parque Natural Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park's mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fábrica de la Luz – Fuente Bonquero loop from Fábrica de la Luz, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This trail offers diverse scenery around the repurposed electricity factory. Local hikers also love the Canillas de Albaida – Ruins of the Olive Mill loop from Canillas de Albaida, a 2.3 miles (3.7 km) trail leading through local landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Hiking around Canillas De Albaida is defined by mountain landscapes, pine forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and more challenging ascents. The routes in Canillas De Albaida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 2,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Canillas De Albaida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Canillas de Albaida?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around Canillas de Albaida, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 6 easy routes, 17 moderate trails, and 11 more challenging options within the region.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Canillas de Albaida?
A: Yes, Canillas de Albaida offers several family-friendly hikes. An easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31559199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canillas de Albaida – Ruins of the Olive Mill loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 3.7 km long and takes just over an hour, perfect for a leisurely outing with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Canillas de Albaida?
A: Many trails around Canillas de Albaida are suitable for dogs, especially those outside the most sensitive protected areas. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, offering diverse mountain landscapes, pine forests, and lush vegetation. You'll encounter rivers like the Rio Turvilla and Rio Cajula, and impressive canyons. Notable peaks and points of interest include Cerro Verde and Fuente Bonquero, providing stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes starting from Canillas de Albaida?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3953783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Saint Anne – Canillas de Albaida loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.5 km circular trail that offers great views.

Q: What are some challenging hiking routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995586362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Lucero – circular from Puerto Blanquillo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.5 km trail within the Natural Park, known for its mountainous terrain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1285422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cómpeta – Cómpeta loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 20 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls or gorges?
A: Yes, the region is known for its dramatic water features. You can explore the Higuerón River Gorge and Waterfall, which offers a scenic walk. The impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is also part of a spectacular hiking route through a gorge, providing an exhilarating experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Canillas de Albaida?
A: The best time for hiking in Canillas de Albaida is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially if you plan to enjoy the rivers and streams.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the trails?
A: Absolutely. Just outside the village, you can find an ancient Roman bridge crossing the Turvilla river and a well-preserved Roman road. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/183798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Pinto Shrine&lt;/a&gt; is another historical point of interest. Additionally, the Ermita de Santa Ana, located at the highest point of the town, offers spectacular panoramic views.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Many trails, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1027083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fábrica de la Luz – Fuente Bonquero loop&lt;/a&gt;, often have designated parking areas at their starting points. The Fábrica de la Luz itself is a popular recreational area and serves as a convenient starting point for various trails, likely offering parking facilities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Canillas de Albaida?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse mountain landscapes, the stunning panoramic views that can extend to the coast, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink near the trails?
A: Canillas de Albaida, being an ideal village for hikers, offers amenities within the village center. Additionally, the Fábrica de la Luz, a repurposed electricity factory, serves as a popular recreational area and is an ideal spot for picnics, often with facilities nearby. Some trails may also lead to nearby villages like Archez, where you can find cafes and pubs.
tips:
We start the route to climb Lucero (1774 m) in Puerto Blanquillo, after traveling an unpaved road for 12 km. It is recommended to take a car with high suspension to avoid problems.

In Puerto Blanquillo, there is no car park as such. We will simply leave the car parked on the side of the road and start the route from there.

We will do the route in a circular way, making the descent through the Puerto del Daire. However, it is also possible to return by the same path that you ascended. The distance is the same.
Start of the trail that leads to the top of Lucero. The information panel tells us that there are 3 km left to the top.
Probably the most attractive peak of the Sierra de Tejeda
After a strenuous 300 meter climb through dense undergrowth, the views will reward you with a beautiful panorama.
